exercises.title: Building longer words
exercises.body: Once you know your consonants and short vowels, you can sound out almost any short word — even ones with a few letters bunched together. Take 𐑛𐑧𐑕𐑒: 𐑛 (d) 𐑧 (e) 𐑕 (s) 𐑒 (k) blends into "desk". Sound it out slowly, then speed up.

exercises.title: Consonant clusters
exercises.body: Some words stack two or three consonants right next to each other, like desk (d-e-s-k) or test (t-e-s-t). Just sound out each letter in order — Shavian never skips a beat, even when English spelling would.

exercises.caption: Two syllables — sound out each part, then say it.
